We utilized a 300 nm thermally grown SiO2 on Si (100) 4’’ wafers for devices isolation, Figure 1, a. The oxide layer is then patterned using photolithographic techniques to have 10 µm diameter holes with 20 µm pitch, followed by a deep reactive ion etching (DRIE) down to 25 µm depth, figure 1, b. Then a spacer formation process is carried out using conformal atomic layer deposition (ALD) of 50 nm

This is because they offer an alternative to complementary metal oxide semiconductor (CMOS) based flash non-volatile memory (NVM) which is approaching scaling limits. For instance, International Technology Roadmap for Semiconductors (ITRS) report

In our p

We have reported foldable memristors fabricated on bulk mono-crystalline silicon (100) using standard CMOS processes. The results show that the basic memristive IV properties of these devices match that of their bulk counterparts with no significant deviation. We believe our process offers a pragmatic step towards densely integrated memristive systems, structurally capable of mimicking biological

rram.text: RRAM device featuring CMOS-technology-process compatibility and high density is a promising candidate for realizing Neural Network (NN) [1], Computing In Memory (CIM)[2] and Neuromorphic systems[3]. In this work, we newly investigate an embedded Non-Volatile Latch Logic structure by TSMC’s pure CMOS Logic 28nm HKMG process to realize a pair of complimentary memristors in n- MOSFET atomic layer dep

rram.text: The 3D structure, layout illustration and circuit schematic of the high performance **e** mbedded **A** rtificial **S** ynaptic **D** evice **(eASD)** are shown in **Fig.1(a)** The cross-sectional transmission electron microscopy (TEM) pictures and EDX of materials are shown in **Fig.1(b)** . The new non-volatile latch consists of one 28nm HKMG n-channel MOSFET as a select transistor and two casca

rram.paper: Pure CMOS embedded Artificial Synaptic Device (eAS
rram.text: The operation steps and characteristics are demonstrated in **Fig.2** . By applying large voltage on **D1** and **D2** terminals, both memristors are initially “Set” to Low Memristance (LMR). Upon writing “1”, MR1 is then “Reset” to High Memristance (HMR) while MR2 still keeps at LMR. The same reset operation is performed on MR2 to complete writing “0” with MR1 keeping at LMR. While the select tra

rram.paper: Pure CMOS embedded Artificial Synaptic Device (eAS
rram.text:
In this work, an embedded artificial synaptic devices ( **eASD** ) fully compatible to pure CMOS HKMG logic process is firstly proposed, showing advantages in neuromorphic computing systems. Both high performance and high density eASD feature high data transmission speed, stability characteristics of output with high writing efficiency and stable read window.

_**Abstract**_ **—Neuromorphic computer will need folded architectural form factor to match brain cortex’s folded pattern for ultra-compact design. In this work, we show a state-of-theart CMOS compatible pragmatic fabricati

SINCEexperimental  their conceptualization and then with their demonstration, memristive devices (popularly memristors) have attracted lots of interest. This is because they offer an alternative to complementary metal oxide semiconductor (CMOS) based flash non-volatile memory (NVM) which is approaching scaling limits. For instance, International Technology Roadmap for Semiconductors (ITRS) report

We have reported foldable memristors fabricated on bulk mono-crystalline silicon (100) using standard CMOS processes. The results show that the basic memristive IV properties of these devices match that of their bulk counterparts with no significant deviation. We believe our process offers a pragmatic step towards densely integrated memristive systems, structurally capable of mimicking biological

neuromorphic_computing.text: The eASD neuromorphic system depicted in **Fig.6** is applying the high density eASD **(HD eASD)** to an array and placing a diode between each output terminal of eASD for rectification. The switch states of the diode are determined and related with the output voltages of adjacent eASDs. The current of each row is summed to the output end **(Yn)** . **Table 1** shows the correlated bias condition

In this work, an embedded artificial synaptic devices ( **eASD** ) fully compatible to pure CMOS HKMG logic process is firstly proposed, showing advantages in neuromorphic computing systems. Both high performance and high density eASD feature high data transmission speed, stability characteristics of output with high writing efficiency and stable read window.
